title: caltha minor subsp. arctica
intro: Caltha minor subsp. arctica, also known as arctic marsh-marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family that is native to the Arctic tundra. The medicinal properties of caltha minor subsp. arctica include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides in the plant. The health benefits of using this plant include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and prevent infection, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine in Arctic regions. Caltha minor subsp. arctica is often used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to promote wound healing and reduce pain.
